name: Mammee apple / mamey (Mammea americana) (food medium)

components.name: water
components.source: Cardona-Marin et al. 2021 (IJASFT 8:242, agriscigroup.us) mean of 6 accessions 84-88 g; USDA SR 'Mammy-apple, raw' NDB 09131
components.evidence: Moisture 84-88 g/100g FW, mean 85.95 (ripe pulp, 6 accessions)

components.name: total carbohydrate
components.source: USDA SR 'Mammy-apple, raw' NDB 09131 (per en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mammea_americana table); Cardona-Marin 2021 range 12-16 g
components.evidence: 12.5 g/100g; dominant carbon pool (free sugars + pectin/soluble fibre)

components.name: dietary fiber (incl. pectin)
components.evidence: 3 g/100g; mamey pulp is notably pectin-rich (cell-wall study PMC11013721)

components.name: sucrose
components.source: da Silva et al. 2020, PMC7024372 (NMR): sucrose 1.51±0.11 mg/g freeze-dried
components.evidence: Dominant free sugar; ranking sucrose>fructose>glucose. Value = NMR free-sugar (1.51 mg/g dry x ~14% dry matter); NMR under-captures soluble sugar so treat as lower bound. Total soluble sugars form much of the 12.5 g carbohydrate.

components.name: D-fructose
components.source: da Silva et al. 2020, PMC7024372 (NMR): fructose 0.85±0.04 mg/g freeze-dried
components.evidence: 2nd free sugar. Value = NMR estimate (0.85 mg/g dry x ~14% DM); lower bound.

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: da Silva et al. 2020, PMC7024372 (NMR): alpha-glucose 0.49±0.02 + beta-glucose 0.40±0.02 mg/g freeze-dried
components.evidence: alpha+beta anomers summed = 0.89 mg/g dry x ~14% DM; lower bound (NMR).

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Yahia & Gutierrez-Orozco 2011 (Postharvest Biology of Tropical Fruits) / acidity ranking in Cardona-Marin 2021
components.evidence: Predominant organic acid of the pulp; sourness ranked citric>malic>tartaric. Mamey is a LOW-acid fruit (ash 0.18 g, mild taste), so absolute citrate is modest; not individually quantified in the literature. Present, amount unmeasured.

components.name: malic acid
components.evidence: Second organic acid; present but unquantified.

components.name: shikimic acid
components.source: da Silva et al. 2020, PMC7024372 (NMR): 0.11±0.01 mg/g freeze-dried

components.name: formic acid
components.source: da Silva et al. 2020, PMC7024372 (NMR): 0.01±0.00 mg/g freeze-dried

components.name: protein (total)
components.source: Cardona-Marin 2021 (0.40-0.47 g, mean ~0.42); USDA SR NDB 09131 (0.5 g)
components.evidence: Low protein; nitrogen source. Free amino-acid profile not individually quantified for this fruit.

components.name: L-lysine
components.evidence: Retained from current profile; part of the 0.42 g protein, individual amount not measured.

components.name: total lipid / fat
components.source: USDA SR NDB 09131 (0.5 g); Cardona-Marin 2021 (<0.2 g, traces)
components.evidence: Minor; not a fatty food. No individual fatty acids reported.

components.name: vitamin A (retinol activity / beta-carotene)
components.evidence: 12 ug RAE; supplied as provitamin-A carotenoids (beta-carotene). Covers current 'Retinol' + 'Beta-Carotene' entries.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: 0.7 mg/100g; consolidates the duplicate Fe2+/Fe3+/Iron current entries.

components.name: copper
components.evidence: Trace essential micronutrient; retained, amount not in USDA entry.

components.name: manganese
components.evidence: Trace essential micronutrient; retained, amount not in USDA entry.

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: Trace; retained, amount not reported for this fruit.

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: Cobalt ion — non-nutritive trace element, not a measured/growth-relevant nutrient of mamey pulp; FooDB mapping artefact

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace/contaminant element, not a growth nutrient of this fruit

remove.name: Molybdate
remove.reason: Ultra-trace element; not measured for this fruit and not a meaningful medium carbon/N/energy component — drop as noise

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: Duplicate/compartment-tagged iron species; consolidated into single 'iron' entry (0.7 mg/100g)

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: Duplicate iron species; consolidated into single 'iron' entry

remove.name: CO2 CO2
remove.reason: Gas exchange species, not a food nutrient of the pulp; FooDB/BiGG environmental artefact

remove.name: O2 O2
remove.reason: Dissolved-gas species, not a compositional nutrient; environmental artefact

remove.name: H2O H2O
remove.reason: Redundant with quantified water entry (kept as 'water' 85.9 g)

remove.name: H+
remove.reason: Proton/pH species, not a compositional nutrient

missing_added: individual free sugars (sucrose>fructose>glucose) with NMR-based amounts
proximates: water, total carbohydrate, dietary fibre/pectin, protein, fat
keep_uncovered: genuine polyphenols/flavonoids — real phytochemicals, no BiGG metabolite
sources: USDA SR 'Mammy-apple (mamey), raw' NDB 09131 (nutrient panel per en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mammea_americana)
da Silva et al. 2020, 'Metabolite Profiling by UPLC-MSE, NMR... Mamey Apple', PMC7024372 (sugars & organic acids by NMR)
Cardona-Marin et al. 2021, 'Nutritional characteristics and phenolic compounds of ripe fruit pulp from six accessions of Mammea americana L.', IJASFT 8:242 (agriscigroup.us) — proximates & phenolics
PMC11013721 — mamey pulp cell-wall / pectin characterisation
notes: Dominant carbon/energy source is the soluble-sugar + pectin fraction of the 12.5 g/100g carbohydrate; free sugars rank sucrose>fructose>glucose (NMR). Mamey is a mild, LOW-acid fruit (ash 0.18 g), organic acids modest with citric predominant (citric>malic>tartaric); citrate/malate present but not individually quantified in the literature. NMR free-sugar amounts are lower bounds (NMR under-captures total soluble sugar). Confidence medium because per-100g absolute sugar and organic-acid values are sparse for this under-studied tropical fruit.
